raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Tardive Dyskinesia (TD) is a movement disorder characterized by random, involuntary, and uncontrolled movements of different muscles in the face, trunk, and extremities.",
    "Tardive Dyskinesia can develop in some people prescribed dopamine receptor blocking agents (DRBAs) to treat chronic mental illness.",
    "Lack of awareness regarding the dynamics of Tardive Dyskinesia reduces the quality of life for those affected by the disorder.",
    "Patients suffering from Tardive Dyskinesia often experience embarrassment due to abnormal and involuntary movements, leading them to withdraw from society.",
    "Tardive Dyskinesia is often unrecognized and patients suffering from the illness are commonly misdiagnosed.",
    "The FDA has approved medications meant to treat Tardive Dyskinesia, providing relief to those who have suffered from this illness.",
    "Medications for Tardive Dyskinesia can have side effects such as dry mouth, sleepiness, and weight gain or loss."
  ]
}
```
